title: Nvidia plans new chip to speed AI processing: WSJ
summary: Nvidia plans to launch a new processor to help OpenAI and other clients run AI systems faster and more efficiently. The chip is for “inference” computing and will debut at Nvidia’s GTC conference. It incorporates a design by startup Groq.

title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman inks deal with Pentagon after break with Anthropic
summary: This deal allows OpenAI to deploy its models within the Pentagon’s classified network under strict safety guardrails. title: Anthropic vows lawsuit over Pentagon ban, slams 'intimidation'
summary: The ban, in the form of a "supply chain risk" designation, came after the company objected to its artificial intelligence technology being used for domestic mass surveillance and fully autonomous weapons.

title: SpaceX could seek IPO valuation of over $1.75 trillion: Bloomberg
summary: Elon Musk’s SpaceX plans a confidential initial public offering that could value the rocket and satellite firm above $1.75 trillion, Bloomberg reported. The filing may come in March, though timing could change. The report said a June IPO is likely, matching that schedule.

title: Trump orders US agencies to stop using Anthropic technology in clash over AI safety
summary: The Trump administration has ordered all U.S. government agencies to stop using artificial intelligence technology from Anthropic and imposed penalties on the company after a dispute over military access to its AI tools.
